howItWorks.intro: This vinyl cutting calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Designs per row = Roll width ÷ (Design width + spacing)
Rows needed = Total designs ÷ Designs per row
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much vinyl do I need?
faq.answer: Calculate based on design size, spacing, and how many fit across your roll width. A 12" wide roll can fit two 5" wide designs per row. Always add 10-15% extra for alignment errors, test cuts, and weeding mistakes.

faq.question: What is the difference between vinyl types?
faq.answer: Adhesive vinyl: for hard surfaces (mugs, signs, decals). Heat transfer vinyl (HTV): for fabrics (t-shirts, bags). Permanent adhesive lasts 6+ years outdoors. Removable is for temporary applications and indoor use.

faq.question: How do I reduce vinyl waste?
faq.answer: Nest designs closely using software (Cricut Design Space, Silhouette Studio). Mirror and rotate to minimize gaps. Cut similar colors in batches. Save scraps for small designs. Use 'print then cut' feature to maximize sheets.
